disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No unilateral weakness, no facial droop, no slurred speech, no true loss of consciousness
trigger_question_examples: Any weakness or drooping on one side of the face or body, or slurred speech?
Did you actually black out, or just feel dizzy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No one-sided weakness, no facial droop, my speech is normal, and I didn't black out.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Tingling is bilateral/symmetric (both hands and perioral) with fast breathing; not a single-limb deficit
trigger_question_examples: Is the tingling in both hands equally, or just one?
Are your lips/around your mouth tingling too, and are you breathing fast?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's both hands equally and around my mouth, and yes I'm breathing fast.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No constant vertigo/imbalance at rest, no severe headache, no double vision (posterior-circulation screen)
trigger_question_examples: Is the room still spinning even when you sit still, or only on standing?
Any severe headache, double vision, or trouble swallowing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It settles when I sit still, no severe headache, no double vision or swallowing trouble.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is my spinal cord being damaged right now, or am I having a stroke?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Reassure accurately that mild early B12 deficiency does not cause sudden overnight spinal-cord or brain damage, screen the stroke red flags, and because she is an older adult with acute dizziness that cannot be examined remotely, keep a low threshold for same-day in-person assessment while coaching breathing for the hyperventilation paraesthesiae.
